picks.final_take: This is the best horse in the field but I’m wrestling over if he overcomes the rail position and if that’s worth betting him under 5-1 odds
picks.writeup:
- Todd Pletcher as trainer and Irad Ortiz is a strong jockey combo
- Has hit the board in all five starts in addition to two straight wins in graded stakes. Only losses are to Paladin who would’ve been the Derby favorite
- Was disappointed to see him draw the rail position as that doesn’t play well for his style (nor is a good spot for anyone) but it least might keep him from going off at a crazy price like 3-1
- Was morning line favorite at 4-1 but people are scared of his post position and now he may go off as second or third favorite
- Has come off the pace while displaying the ability to work through traffic and finish on the outside if he needs all while winning handily down the stretch
- No notes for this guy he looks legit. Just will be a matter of what price he goes off at
- Hands down best horse, if he can keep away from getting too far behind at the start I think he can win

picks.final_take: Guy hasn’t been in a real race. Step up in competition will not suit him. A top 15 finish would be a success.
picks.writeup:
- Hasn’t raced anyone nor looked impressive in said races
- Wood memorial win was similar to if Jack Leland laced them up and won the Charlotte Turkey Trot Kids Mile Fun Run

picks.last_race: 4th Santa Anita Derby to So Happy
picks.writeup: - Nothing to read here. Horse is mid

picks.final_take: Don’t buy the Bob Baffert Kool-Aid
picks.writeup:
- Bob Baffert Horse (I Hate Baffert). He will get money (odds will come down) just because of Baffert. My weekend in Louisville will be ruined if he wins. Half Joking.
- Joking aside I think this horse is awful I think he truthfully should go off closer to 75-1
- If your sportsbook offers H2H horse results fade him every way you can

picks.final_take: There is a better chance that “We Like To Party” by K.A.L (go check him out on spotify, up and coming talent in Atlanta) hits triple platinum than Right to Party wins on saturday
picks.writeup:
- Historically best post position to break from so he has that going for him, but alas, this horse sucks. Waste of a good draw
- Has hit the board in all four starts but against nobody
- Weaved in and out actually impressively in the Wood but the Derby horses wont be as easily passed
- Regressed in each race with the added distance in each

picks.last_race: Won Florida Derby in photo over Puma and a neck in front of Chief Wallabee
picks.final_take: I command Commandment to lay a dud on Saturday. Of the <10-1 odds horses I’m least inclined to buy this one. Toss and go with other top horses
picks.writeup:
- Coming off 4 unbeaten races with multiple of which over Chief Wallabee and The Puma
- None of which blowing competition away
- Neck and neck with Chief Wallabee in each of the past couple races. Little difference between the two
- Drew a perfect post position
- I’m going to fade this horse so im hoping the good post just drives down his odds

picks.final_take: He is a big wild card here. If you use a foreign horse in your slips use him, but I wont use any real estate of mine
picks.writeup:
- I really came into this writeup wanting to fall in love with him but I haven’t
- He has three impressive starts and visually pleasing wins
- Set a track record in his last start
- Will have to transition from the sandier dirt and right-turn tracks from Japan to the course at Churchill Downs
- Not a ton of time in America to acclimate and prep
- Doesn’t feel like the hype the first Japanese winning horse should/will have prior to the race. Probably the broadcast will talk him up and make him tempting to viewers
- I think the most likely of the foreign horses to make a splash

picks.final_take: Toss. Better horses to bet on, especially at how low his odds have gotten
picks.writeup:
- Looked impressive in the Santa Anita and is the best west coast horse in this Derby
- However I think it was a weak crop out west this year and he won’t be able to compete with the big boys
- High speed ratings yes, but as I explain every year these get inflated due to the Cali circuit has fast dirt and favors speed horses for the ratings

picks.last_race: 2nd in Florida Derby losing photo finish to Commandment
picks.final_take: I think he finishes in the 4-8 range, though I wont use him in any slips. Better horses to take a chance on
picks.writeup:
- Most impressive finish is beating Further Ado in second to last outing
- Though as you will read Further Ado was in the middle of a flu game that day
- Otherwise he also already got beaten by Renegade, Chief Wallabee, and Commandment (by a nose)
- He is no better or worse than Commandment whomst I already tossed
- Rumors are swirling that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce recently just invested in this horse as 10% owners. If true that means not only will they be at the track and on tv way too much, but this horse will take on a lot of extra money

- Beat Six Speed by a neck in the UAE after coming from a stalking position
- Japanese horse. Again, these things have never won + when they’ve come close, they’ve been all the rage and talk of town. This horse is not that.
- A lot of people though think he’s the better of the Japanese horses, though I do not
- He get to Kentucky almost immediately after the UAE race to acclimate which should be a positive for him

picks.final_take: Most average horse in the field. Best case he pops off for a fifth but I think equal shot he finishes bottom 5. Pass
picks.writeup:
- Beat a poor field in the Virginia Derby, but had an inspiring kick at the end to pull away
- Going to be overlooked by a lot of people, myself included, because of his outing before that dropping an egg and losing to comparable horses soundly

picks.last_race: 3rd in Florida Derby only losing by a neck to both Commandment and The Puma
picks.final_take: I don’t see him having the closing speed to win the Derby but I like him in some exotics
picks.writeup:
- Of the three Florida Derby horses I think he is most deserving of a bet, he got stuck running through a bad trip in that race and still almost won
- Lightly raced horse though did not race as a 2 year old
- Decent post position draw here
- Using blinkers for first time in career is concerning

picks.last_race: 2nd in Santa Anita Derby to So Happy
picks.final_take: Worse than So Happy who I already tossed. Toss this nerd too. If Baffert is walking in front of me after the race as a winner please shoot me a get well soon text.
picks.writeup:
- (See So Happy’s write up)
- Very similar tier horse who might just be a step behind So Happy
- This is Bob Baffert’s best horse so it is my #1 enemy
- A lot of other handicappers like the Derby size and length to suit him well but I just don’t see anything to rave about here.
- Cali speed figures are slightly inflated imo, so don’t assume he’s this fast
- I hope he takes a lot of money that will come back to me via another horse

picks.final_take: Only has two races to his name and that hasn’t produced a winner in 100ish years. He will be a popular pick but there will be better options. Feel free to use underneath if you fall in love with him
picks.writeup:
- Lightly raced horse with only two starts which is seen as historically too little in order to win
- He will take money though because it is popular to go with the LA winner since it is the closest in the long distance they have to run in the Derby
- I don’t think he was that much better than Pavlovian who was a long odds in that race and held on to his pace the entire race from the front
- He may go off closer to 12-1 but I don’t think he’s worth the hype you will hear come May 2nd, mostly because it is tough to bet on inexperience, especially with non-dominant (though 2 and 0 in two starts) experience

picks.final_take: I genuinely believe he will be on the lead during the last turn but the question will be how many catch him, if he stays in top 5 contention or drops more
picks.writeup:
- Overly raced horse and I don’t like horses coming in with more than 5 races, let alone this guy with 10. And he’s only won 2 of those. Albeit he has done better as his distance has extended and as a pace setter who can handle distance the added length at the Kentucky Derby may be good for him?
- Three races ago he had an awful start and then worked through the pack and even chased down the leaders on the final stretch even though finishing 3rd. Showed versatility.
- I do like him to outrun his odds given he’s improved with the extra distance and with a reason in his previous race for a bad result
- Getting the 1 post in the LA derby may have propped him up there for his style
- He held the pace at the front the entire way in the Louisiana Derby and was just beat down the stretch by Emerging Market by a nose. He didn’t lose his pace in the longer mile and 3/16 race which is a good indication he can go the distance as a pace setter
- If he can set a slowish first half of the race he would set himself up for a final stretch to fend off the rest of the pack

picks.final_take: Not a generational foreign horse or else we would be hearing about it. He may be on the lead for the first half then finish 16th
picks.writeup:
- One of the Foreign horses shipping to the Derby. This has never produced a winner
- Largely is expected to gun to the front and run until he gets tired
- Distance will be a major concern here

picks.final_take: Torn between him and Renegade. May come down to a coin flip between the two. He could absolutely win and should be used in all exotics
picks.writeup:
- Look absolutely dominant winning by like 15 lengths while extending a lead down the stretch
- Lost his other 2026 start prior to this one to The Puma in a clean race where he just wasn’t able to outrun everyone at the end
- My research provides special insight that he was sick during this Tampa Bay Downs which led to his non perfect start, though he finished just behind The Puma. This is the kind of investigating journalism you subscribers are welcome for
- Oh no he got second only by a neck to another horse that will go off in the top 3 betting favorites
- Considering he might be getting downgraded due to his sick race 2nd place, he might be the real deal
- The question will be if he legitimately took that big of a jump in his last start or if it was just an impressive win against bad horses
- If the former, he could absolutely wear the roses after the race on Saturday
- However another tidbit of information here. As I am high on Further Ado and think he’s a great horse, Irad Ortiz had ridden him earlier and was given first look to mount him for the Derby but Irad chose Renegade as he’s personally chasing his first elusive Derby win. The jockeys absolutely can tell when riding multiple horses which ones they trust more.

picks.final_take: This guy is my long shot pick but I feel like I find a profile similar to his every year I try to convince myself into. Use underneath and exotics as he should be bred to close this race really well. I will also hold a win ticket for him so I don’t miss out. Ill also probably hold a show ticket for him as well.
picks.writeup:
- I think this horse has a much better chance than his odds indicate. He checks a lot of boxes that work well in the Derby, 4 career starts and not overworked, has improved with the added distance in every race, and makes his move on the last turn
- In the Louisiana Derby he started well in last after the opening gate (common theme for him in each race) then rallied back in the last turn and actually looked like he could’ve had the most straight-line speed until his momentum got killed as he had to change from outermost lane to the rail for the last stretch
- Early workouts at Churchill Downs indicate they might be pushing him to start quicker in the derby would be a tall task to switch strategies from closing to pressing the pace given the stakes
- He is worth a flier here to hit the board and to use underneath in exotics given he is a strong closer
- Con would be trainer is Cherie Devaux and not one of the main guys printing winners

picks.final_take: Too many cons, probably fair odds will be 15 or 20-1, but there will be better horses to choose. Feel free to use underneath if want
picks.writeup:
- Never has raced on dirt after running a pair early in his career on grass and then most recently on synthetic track. Usually is a red flag just from the unknown on how they will transition. I air on it not being the worst thing in the world as the horses often train on dirt and most definitely get workouts in at the track the gain comfort before the Derby. Rich Strike came from synthetic track background before beating me and winning the only other Derby I went to, so not going to overlook because of that again.
- Thing that gives me pause is him losing to Great white in his prior race who separately got demolished by Further Ado in his prep race. So when comparing across similar opponents I don’t think he’s my guy
- That said – he does look visually impressive and gains speed while closing, so if he transitions to dirt here you could make a case for him.
